Course Title: Explainability: Can You Justify AI Decisions?
Category: Finance & Professional Services: Banking, Insurance, Accounting, Consulting, Legal, Real Estate
Who is this for: Compliance officers, risk managers, loan officers, underwriters, legal counsel, financial advisors, insurance agents, auditors, business analysts, product managers
Team or Dept this is for: Compliance, Risk Management, Legal, Audit, Operations, Product Development, Customer Service
Description: Why “black box” AI is a compliance problem.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with how AI or automated decision-making tools are used in financial or professional services is helpful, though no technical background is required.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key reasons why "black box" AI poses a compliance risk in regulated industries such as banking, insurance, and legal services, and explain the core principles of AI explainability in plain, accessible terms. You will also be able to apply practical frameworks for justifying AI-driven decisions to colleagues, clients, and regulators, helping your organisation meet its transparency and accountability obligations with confidence.
